content.text: The archives are here. The sanity check needs `povdoc/include`, so I need both POVSRC and POVDOC. Let me download them and read the README.

input.command:
cd /tmp
base="https://www.povray.org/ftp/pub/povray/Old-Versions/Official-2.2"
curl -sL -o POVSRC.TAR.Z "$base/POVSRC.TAR.Z"
curl -sL -o POVDOC.TAR.Z "$base/POVDOC.TAR.Z"
curl -sL -o POVSCN.TAR.Z "$base/POVSCN.TAR.Z"
curl -sL -o README2.2 "$base/README2.2"
ls -la POVSRC.TAR.Z POVDOC.TAR.Z POVSCN.TAR.Z
file POVSRC.TAR.Z 2>/dev/null || echo "no file cmd"
echo "=== README head ==="
head -60 README2.2

content.text:
===== unix.mak =====
# Makefile for Persistence of Vision Raytracer
# This file is released to the public domain.
#
#
# MAKE Macros and Such...
#

#***************************************************************
#*
#*                      UNIX Makefile
#*
#***************************************************************

# The exact options may depend on your compiler.  Feel free to modify
# these as required.
# The cc compiler on a HP9000 likes the -Aa option for all files except unix.c
# The gcc compiler is usually satisfied with these options.

OBJ	= o
MACHINE_OBJ	= unix.$(OBJ)
CFLAGS=		-c -O
LFLAGS =	-o povray -O
CC =            cc

# Make's implicit rules for making a .o file from a .c file...
#
.c.o :
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $*.c


POVOBJS = povray.$(OBJ) bezier.$(OBJ) blob.$(OBJ) bound.$(OBJ) boxes.$(OBJ)  \
	  camera.$(OBJ) colour.$(OBJ) cones.$(OBJ) csg.$(OBJ) discs.$(OBJ)   \
	  dump.$(OBJ) express.$(OBJ) gif.$(OBJ) gifdecod.$(OBJ)              \
	  hfield.$(OBJ) iff.$(OBJ) image.$(OBJ) lighting.$(OBJ)              \
	  matrices.$(OBJ) normal.$(OBJ) objects.$(OBJ) parse.$(OBJ)          \
	  pigment.$(OBJ) planes.$(OBJ) point.$(OBJ) poly.$(OBJ)              \
	  quadrics.$(OBJ) raw.$(OBJ) ray.$(OBJ) render.$(OBJ) spheres.$(OBJ) \
	  targa.$(OBJ) texture.$(OBJ) tokenize.$(OBJ) triangle.$(OBJ)        \
	  txttest.$(OBJ) vect.$(OBJ) $(MACHINE_OBJ)


povray:	$(POVOBJS)
	$(CC) $(LFLAGS) $(POVOBJS) -lm

This file contains the documentation specific to UNIX systems.  Due to the
wide variety of UNIX systems available, POV-Ray may not compile directly

we have access to.  If you have problems compiling, try checking with a
local UNIX guru first.  If you still have problems, contact the POV-Ray team.

If you are trying to compile POV-Ray on an unsupported platform, the UNIX
version is the best one to use as a base.


Differences from original distribution:
   The original distribution of POV-Ray is in ZIP format.  For UNIX,
   this has been converted to compressed TAR format.  In addition, the UNIX

Executables:

Build complete and verified. Summary:

**What I did**
- Installed the missing toolchain (`build-essential` — no compiler was present).
- Downloaded the official POV-Ray 2.2 archives from `povray.org/ftp/.../Old-Versions/Official-2.2/`: `POVSRC.TAR.Z` (source), `POVDOC.TAR.Z` (needed for `povdoc/include`), and `POVSCN.TAR.Z`.
- Extracted all to `/app/povray-2.2`.
- Followed the documented Unix build: created `/app/povray-2.2/build`, copied `source/*` + `machine/unix/unix.c` (null display driver, ideal for headless), used `gccconf.h` as `config.h` and `unix.mak` as `makefile`, built with `gcc`.
- Installed the binary to `/usr/local/bin/povray`.

**Two fixes required for a modern gcc/clean run**
1. Link error — a case mismatch in the original code: `config.h` calls `unix_init_povray()` but `unix.c` defined `unix_init_POVRAY`. Renamed the definition to match.
2. Non-deterministic exit code — `main` is `void` with an empty `FINISH_POVRAY`, so it fell off the end returning garbage (saw 0/4/235). Defined `FINISH_POVRAY exit(0);` in `config.h` (exactly what that macro is documented for). Exit is now a stable 0.

**Verification**
- Sanity check (`+L.../povdoc/include +I/app/deps/illum1.pov +O/dev/null +P -V`) completes with exit 0 across repeated runs and prints full rendering statistics (91,599 rays, 100×100).
- Confirmed a real render to TGA produces a valid 30,018-byte 100×100 uncompressed image.

`/app/deps/illum1.pov` was not modified.
